title: Conjecture 1.1 (Kelly–Postle)
statement_text: For every triangle-free graph there exists a probability distribution on its independent sets such that every vertex $v\in V(G)$ appears with probability at least $(1-o(1))\frac{\ln d_{G}(v)}{d_{G}(v)}$ in a random independent set chosen from the distribution. Here, the $o(1)$ term represents any function that tends to $0$ as the degree grows.
context_text: Posed by Kelly and Postle in 2018 as a 'local strengthening' of Shearer's bounds and a degree-sequence generalization of Molloy's bound for fractional coloring. The distribution it posits recovers Shearer's second (stronger) lower bound on the independence number up to lower-order terms, while additionally guaranteeing that every individual vertex is covered with high probability by the random independent set.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full: for every triangle-free graph there exists a probability distribution on independent sets such that every vertex $v$ is contained with probability $(1-o(1))\frac{\ln d_{G}(v)}{d_{G}(v)}$.
notes: Referred to in [35] as Conjecture 2.2 / Proposition 1.4; described in the abstract as the 'main conjecture' resolved by this paper.

title: Conjecture 1.3 (Cames van Batenburg et al.)
statement_text: As $n\rightarrow\infty$, every triangle-free graph on $n$ vertices has fractional chromatic number at most $(\sqrt{2}+o(1))\sqrt{\frac{n}{\ln n}}$.
context_text: Raised in 2018 by Cames van Batenburg, de Joannis de Verclos, Kang, and Pirot, motivated by Erdős's question on the maximum chromatic number of triangle-free graphs and its connection to the Ramsey number $R(3,k)$. Prior to this paper, the best known upper bound on $\chi_f$ for triangle-free graphs on $n$ vertices was $(2+o(1))\sqrt{\frac{n}{\ln n}}$, established by the same authors.
attributed_to: Cames van Batenburg, de Joannis de Verclos, Kang, and Pirot
paper_contribution: Confirms the conjecture by combining the paper's proof of Conjecture 1.1 with a connection between the two conjectures established by Kelly and Postle [35, Proposition 5.2].
notes: Corresponds to Conjecture 4.3 in [12] (Cames van Batenburg et al., Electron. J. Combin. 2020).

title: Conjecture 1.7
context_text: Motivated by Harris's conjecture [27] that every triangle-free $d$-degenerate graph satisfies $\chi_f(G)\leq O(\frac{d}{\ln d})$ and by Molloy's bound $(1+o(1))\frac{\Delta(G)}{\ln\Delta(G)}$ in terms of the maximum degree. Since the spectral radius $\rho(G)$ is sandwiched between the degeneracy and the maximum degree, Theorem 1.6 of this paper (a new spectral upper bound on $\chi_f$) is presented as a first step toward interpolating between these two regimes.
